• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

ESDSWRF Oat-Pea Polyculture Manuscript Analysis

PME

29 May, 2024, 16:07

Overview

Analysis for the oat-pea polyculture experiment. This experiment compared the performance of oat, pea, and oat-pea in a mix. Performance was measured by yield, biomass, and stability. The experiment was planted on two dates, late April and June 1st, to simulate site-years. The early planting date included a 3-way, oat-pea-flax mixture.

Major questions are?

  1. Does the oat-pea mixture yield more per acre than monocrops? This will be assessed by calculating land equivalent ratios.
  2. Is the oat-pea mixture more stable across weather patterns than the monocrops? This will be assessed as change in yield across planting dates.
  3. Does the addition of flax hinder oat and pea development?

The design is split plot with four blocks. The main plots are planting date with crop mixture as subplots. Therefore, the error structure is ~(1|BLOCK) + (1|BLOCK:PLANTING) + residuals (error terms for block and planting date within block).

Load

libs = c(
  'Matrix', # must install manually for some reason
  'multcompView', # also must install manually
  'lme4',
  'car',
  'ggplot2',
  'ggtext',
  'multcomp',
  'emmeans',
  'tidyr',
  'magrittr',
  'here',
  'wesanderson',
  'gridExtra'
)

for (i in libs) {
  if (!require(i, character.only=TRUE)) {
    renv::install(i)
    library(i, character.only=TRUE)
  }
}
sapply(libs, function(x) paste(packageVersion(x), collapse='.')) %>% 
  c(R=paste(R.Version()[c('major', 'minor')], collapse='.'), .) %>% 
  as.matrix %>% 
  set_colnames('Version')
##              Version   
## R            "4.3.2"   
## Matrix       "1.6.5"   
## multcompView "0.1.10"  
## lme4         "1.1.35.3"
## car          "3.1.2"   
## ggplot2      "3.5.1"   
## ggtext       "0.1.2"   
## multcomp     "1.4.25"  
## emmeans      "1.10.2"  
## tidyr        "1.3.1"   
## magrittr     "2.0.3"   
## here         "1.0.1"   
## wesanderson  "0.3.7"   
## gridExtra    "2.3"
colors = wes_palette('Darjeeling1', 5)[-3]

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')
in_dir = 'Data'
in_df = 'analysis dataframe.csv'
out_dir = 'Results'

df = here(in_dir, in_df) %>% 
  read.csv

names(df) %<>% gsub('DATE', 'PLANTING', .) %>% 
  gsub('PLANTING_PLANTING', 'PLANTING_DATE', .)
facts = c(
  'YEAR', 'LOCATION', 'SID', 'PLOT', 'REP', 'TREATMENT', 'PLANTING', 'CROP', 'ROW', 'COL', 'BARCODE',
  'PLANTING_DATE'
)

df[, facts] %<>% lapply(as.factor)

Functions

se = function(x) sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))

get_mean_se = function(ff, data) {
  aggregate(ff, 
            data=data, 
            FUN=function(x) c(MEAN=mean(x),
                              SE=se(x))) %>% 
    do.call(data.frame, .)
}

group_Y = function(data, resp, pred, bump=0.05) {
  ff = paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% 
    formula
  out = aggregate(ff, data=data, function(x) mean(x) + se(x))
  names(out) %<>% gsub(resp, 'Y', .)
  out[, 'Y'] = out[, 'Y'] + bump*max(out[, 'Y'])
  return(out)
}

gg_formatter = function(x) {
  out = x + 
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid=element_blank(), 
          axis.ticks=element_line(color='black', linewidth=0.1), 
          axis.ticks.length=unit(0.3, units='mm'),
          legend.position='bottom',
          axis.title.x=element_markdown(),
          axis.title.y=element_markdown())
  return(out)
}

text_sizer = function(x, size=8) {
  require(ggplot2)
  out = x +
    theme(legend.text=element_text(size=size-1),
        strip.text=element_markdown(size=size),
        axis.title=element_markdown(size=size-1),
        axis.text=element_text(size=size-1),
        text=element_text(size=size),
        legend.box.spacing=margin(0.1))
  return(out)
}

QA/QC

Treatments contingency table

with(df, table(CROP, PLANTING))
##               PLANTING
## CROP           early late
##   oat              4    4
##   oat-pea          4    4
##   oat-pea-flax     4    0
##   pea              4    4

Missing values of different measurements.

sapply(df, function(x) sum(is.na(x))) %>% 
  .[.>0]
##    OAT_HEIGHT_FLOWER    PEA_HEIGHT_FLOWER   FLAX_HEIGHT_FLOWER 
##                    8                    8                   24 
##  OAT_HEIGHT_MATURITY  PEA_HEIGHT_MATURITY FLAX_HEIGHT_MATURITY 
##                   16                   16                   24 
## FLAX_MATURE_PLANTING            PREDATION      PREDATION_EATEN 
##                   28                   12                   12 
##   PREDATION_NOTEATEN      BUGVAC_RICHNESS     BUGVAC_ABUNDANCE 
##                   12                   12                   12 
##               OAT_CP               PEA_CP              OAT_ADF 
##                    8                    8                    8 
##              PEA_ADF              OAT_NDF              PEA_NDF 
##                    8                    8                    8 
##              OAT_TDN              PEA_TDN               OAT_ME 
##                    8                    8                    8 
##               PEA_ME              OAT_NEl              PEA_NEl 
##                    8                    8                    8 
##              OAT_NEg              PEA_NEg              OAT_NEm 
##                    8                    8                    8 
##              PEA_NEm               OAT_DE               PEA_DE 
##                    8                    8                    8 
##              OAT_DDM              PEA_DDM              OAT_DMI 
##                    8                    8                    8 
##              PEA_DMI              OAT_RFV              PEA_RFV 
##                    8                    8                    8

Missing some height data (late planting). Others are consistent with experimental design.

Very rough outlier check.

is_data = sapply(df, is.numeric)

tt = cbind(df[, c('CROP', 'PLANTING', 'REP')], 
           df[, is_data]) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols=where('is.numeric'))
tt[, 'value'] %<>% sapply(function(x) ifelse(x==0, NA, x))
ggplot(tt,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=value,
           color=PLANTING, 
           shape=REP)) +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales='free') +
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:4) +
  geom_point(size=3)
## Warning: Removed 559 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

Outliers:

  • Weed biomass, which is to be expected due to patchiness.That specific part of the field was especially weedy.
  • Predation seems bimodal, so tough to analyze.
  • Pea harvest index - late planting, oat-pea, replicate A. This is worth investigating/re-measuring.

Treatment Properties

Stand counts

aggregate(STAND_OAT ~ CROP + PLANTING, 
          data=df, 
          mean)
aggregate(STAND_PEA ~ CROP + PLANTING, data=df, mean)
aggregate(STAND_FLAX ~ CROP + PLANTING, data=df, mean)

Weather

in_wet = 'Jan1thruSept26_2023.csv'

wet = here(in_dir, 
           in_wet) %>% 
  read.csv

names(wet) = c(
  'YEAR',
  'DOY',
  'DATE',
  'MINT',
  'MAXT',
  'AVGT',
  'PPT')

wet$`NA` = NULL
wet$PPT = wet$PPT * 25.4
wet$DATE %<>% as.Date(format='%m/%d/%Y')

planting_dates = df$PLANTING_DATE %>% 
  as.Date(format='%m/%d/%Y') %>% 
  unique %>% 
  sort %>% 
  set_names(c('early', 'late'))

flowering_dates = c(early='2023-06-16', 
                    late='2023-07-17') %>% 
  as.Date

harvest_dates = c(early='2023-07-17',
                  late='2023-08-22') %>% 
  as.Date()

season=c(early='early',
         late='late') %>% 
  lapply(function(x) c(planting=planting_dates[[x]],
                       flowering=flowering_dates[[x]],
                       harvest=harvest_dates[[x]]))
subset_weather = function(dates, weather) {
  out = subset(weather, DATE >= dates[1] & DATE <= dates[2])
  return(out)
}

GDD = function(low, high, base=0, ceiling=Inf, cumulative=TRUE) {
  x = cbind(low, high)
  x[x<base] = base
  x[x>ceiling] = ceiling

  gdd = (x[,2] + x[,1]) / 2
  gdd = gdd - base
  
  if (cumulative) gdd = cumsum(gdd)
  
  return(gdd)
}

get_weather = function(planting=c('early', 'late'), weather) {
  dates = season[[planting]][c('planting', 'harvest')]
  x = subset_weather(dates, weather)
  
  out = data.frame(
    PLANTING=planting,
    DAP = seq_len(nrow(x)),
    GDD = GDD(x$MINT, x$MAXT, cumulative=TRUE),
    CUM_PPT = cumsum(x$PPT)
  )
  return(out)
}
early = get_weather('early', wet)
late = get_weather('late', wet)

pltdf = rbind(early, late) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols=c('GDD', 'CUM_PPT'))

pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>%
  as.character %>%
  prettyplanting[.]

prettynames = c(
  CUM_PPT = 'Cumulative<br>Precipitation [mm]',
  GDD = 'Cumulative<br>Growing Degree Days [C]'
)
pltdf$prettynames = prettynames[pltdf$name] %>% 
  factor(levels=prettynames)


flower_DAP = lapply(season, function(x) x['flowering']-x['planting'])
flower_DAP = names(flower_DAP) %>% 
  lapply(function(x) subset(pltdf, PLANTING==x & DAP==flower_DAP[[x]])) %>% 
  do.call(rbind, .)

Plot Fig 1 - Weather

plt = ggplot(pltdf,
             aes(x=DAP,
                 y=value,
                 color=prettyplanting)) +
  facet_wrap(~prettynames,
             scales='free_y') +
  scale_color_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  # geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0),
  #            color='lightgray',
  #            linewidth=0.05) +
  geom_path(linewidth=0.2) +
  geom_point(data=flower_DAP,
             shape=10,
             size=2,
             stroke=0.2) +
  labs(x='Days After Planting',
       y=NULL,
       color=NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks=element_line(color='black', linewidth=0.1), 
        axis.ticks.length=unit(0.3, units='mm'),
        legend.position='bottom')

plt

fig1 = text_sizer(plt)
fig1

here(out_dir, 
     'Figure 1 - Weather.jpeg') %>% 
  jpeg(height=2.5, width=4, units='in', res=600)
fig1
dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Productivity

Total Yield

The formula is:

resp = 'COMBO_YIELD_HAND'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula %T>%
  print
## COMBO_YIELD_HAND ~ CROP * PLANTING + (1 | REP/PLANTING)
## <environment: 0x000001d973bdf3b8>
mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

A marginal interaction between crop and planting date. Crop has a main effect, as does planting.

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)
ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

In general, oats yielded better than mixes and peas. The late planting was more productive - especially for the monocultures. For the early planting, the mix seemed as productive as the oat monoculture. Whether flax affected oat-pea yields is uncertain.

Yields were quite poor in all cases. Will need to compare to oat-clover yields.

This suggests that the oat-pea mix is more stable across weather patterns.

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE)
emeans
emeans1 = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=LETTERS,
      reverse=TRUE)
emeans1
colnames(emeans1) %<>% gsub('.group', '.GROUP', .)
keep = c('PLANTING', 'CROP', '.group')

has_flax = grepl('flax', emeans$CROP)
emeans[has_flax, '.group'] = ''

emeans %<>% 
  .[, keep] %>% 
  merge(emeans1)

emeans[, '.group'] = with(emeans, 
                          paste0(trimws(.GROUP), 
                                 trimws(.group)))
emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  pwpm
## 
## PLANTING = early
##                 oat oat-pea oat-pea-flax    pea
## oat          [2169]  0.9807       0.9198 <.0001
## oat-pea        76.6  [2092]       0.9940 0.0002
## oat-pea-flax  127.8    51.2       [2041] 0.0003
## pea          1258.2  1181.6       1130.4 [ 911]
## 
## PLANTING = late
##                 oat oat-pea oat-pea-flax    pea
## oat          [2827]  0.1219           NA 0.0014
## oat-pea         426  [2402]           NA 0.0856
## oat-pea-flax     NA      NA       [  NA]     NA
## pea             891     466           NA [1936]
## 
## Row and column labels: CROP
## Upper triangle: P values   adjust = "tukey"
## Diagonal: [Estimates] (emmean) 
## Lower triangle: Comparisons (estimate)   earlier vs. later

Plot

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')
grp = 'CROP+PLANTING'



annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans, all.y=TRUE)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()

grpY = group_Y(resp, grp, data=df)
annotate_df %<>% merge(grpY, all.x=TRUE)

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', 'COMBO_YIELD_HAND') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  rbind(
    data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               COMBO_YIELD_HAND=0
               )
  )
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=COMBO_YIELD_HAND,
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger,
               linewidth=0.1) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=2.5,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Total Grain Yield [kg ha<sup>-1</sup>]',
       fill=NULL)
plt %<>% gg_formatter()
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Format for Export

fig2_data = list()
fig2_annotate = list()

annotate_df$RESP=resp
pltdf$RESP = resp
names(pltdf) %<>% gsub(resp, 'Y', .)

fig2_data[[resp]] = pltdf
fig2_annotate[[resp]] = annotate_df
fig2_yield = text_sizer(plt)
fig2_yield
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Total Biomass

resp = 'COMBO_BIOMASS_MATURITY'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

No interaction between crop and planting date. Crop has a main effect, as does planting.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE)
emeans
emeans1 = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=LETTERS,
      reverse=TRUE)
colnames(emeans1) %<>% gsub('.group', '.GROUP', .)

emeans1
keep = c('PLANTING', 'CROP', '.group')

has_flax = grepl('flax', emeans$CROP)
emeans[has_flax, '.group'] = ''

emeans %<>% 
  .[, keep] %>% 
  merge(emeans1)

emeans[, '.group'] = with(emeans, 
                          paste0(trimws(.GROUP), 
                                 trimws(.group)))
prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans,
        all.y=TRUE)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()

grpY = group_Y(resp, grp, data=df)
annotate_df %<>% merge(grpY, all.x=TRUE)

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger,
               linewidth=0.1) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=2.5,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Total Biomass [kg ha<sup>-1</sup>]',
       fill=NULL)
plt %<>% gg_formatter
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Format for Export

annotate_df$RESP=resp
annotate_df$prettyplanting %<>% gsub('Planting ', '', .)
pltdf$RESP = resp
pltdf$prettyplanting %<>% gsub('Planting ', '', .)
names(pltdf) %<>% gsub(resp, 'Y', .)

fig2_data[[resp]] = pltdf
fig2_annotate[[resp]] = annotate_df

fig2_biomass = text_sizer(plt)
fig2_biomass

Harvest Index

resp = 'COMBO_HI'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

No interaction between crop and planting date. Crop has a main effect, as does planting.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Pea had a higher HI in planting B. Oat and oat/pea had a higher HI in planting A.

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()

grpY = group_Y(resp, grp, data=df)
annotate_df %<>% merge(grpY, all.x=TRUE)

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Harvest Index [kg kg<sup>-1</sup>]',
       fill=NULL)
plt %<>% gg_formatter
  
plt

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Plotted Land Equivalent Ratios

means_df = subset(df, CROP %in% c('oat', 'pea'))
oat_means = aggregate(OAT_YIELD_HAND ~ PLANTING + CROP, data=means_df, mean)
pea_means = aggregate(PEA_YIELD_HAND ~ PLANTING + CROP, data=means_df, mean)
means_df = merge(oat_means, pea_means)
means_df %<>% rbind(
  data.frame(
    PLANTING = c('early', 'late') %>% 
      factor(levels=levels(means_df$PLANTING)),
    CROP = c(NA, NA),
    OAT_YIELD_HAND= c(0, 0),
    PEA_YIELD_HAND = c(0, 0)
  )
)

annotate_df = data.frame(
  PLANTING = c('early', 'early', 'late', 'late'),
  x = c(350, 1800, 500, 2000),
  y = c(50, 1000, 100, 2100),
  LABEL = c('LER < 1', 'LER > 1', 'LER < 1', 'LER > 1')
)

means_df$prettyplanting = means_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]

annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]

df$prettyplanting = df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
plt = ggplot(df,
       aes(x=OAT_YIELD_HAND,
           y=PEA_YIELD_HAND,
           color=CROP,
           shape=CROP)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(18, 17, 15, 16)) +
  facet_wrap(~prettyplanting, scales='free') +
  geom_polygon(data=means_df,
            aes(x=OAT_YIELD_HAND,
                y=PEA_YIELD_HAND),
            inherit.aes=FALSE,
            fill='lightgray',
            color=NA) +
  geom_point(size=1.5,
             alpha=0.7) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
             aes(x=x,
                 y=y, 
                 label=LABEL),
            size=2.5,
            inherit.aes=FALSE) +
  labs(x='Oat Yield [kg ha<sup>-1</sup>]',
       y='Pea Yield [kg ha<sup>-1</sup>]',
       color=NULL,
       shape=NULL) +
  theme_minimal()
plt %<>% gg_formatter()

scatterplot approach. Points above the line are overyielding. So the early planting date overyielded.

Format for export

fig2_LER = text_sizer(plt)
fig2_LER

Calculated LER

LER = sum(y_poly/y_mono)

yieldnames = c('OAT', 'PEA') %>% 
  paste0('_YIELD_HAND')

cols = c('REP', 'PLANTING', 'CROP', yieldnames)

ler = df[, cols] %>% 
  subset(CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ler = pivot_wider(ler, 
                  names_from='CROP',
                  values_from=yieldnames)
## Warning: Using an external vector in selections was deprecated in tidyselect 1.1.0.
## ℹ Please use `all_of()` or `any_of()` instead.
##   # Was:
##   data %>% select(yieldnames)
## 
##   # Now:
##   data %>% select(all_of(yieldnames))
## 
## See <https://tidyselect.r-lib.org/reference/faq-external-vector.html>.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
ler$OAT_FRAC = with(ler, `OAT_YIELD_HAND_oat-pea`/`OAT_YIELD_HAND_oat`)
ler$PEA_FRAC = with(ler, `PEA_YIELD_HAND_oat-pea`/`PEA_YIELD_HAND_pea`)
ler$LER = with(ler, OAT_FRAC + PEA_FRAC)
to_drop = grepl('YIELD_HAND', colnames(ler))
ler %<>% .[, !to_drop]
resp = 'LER'
pred = 'PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP)'

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=ler)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

No difference in LER across planting dates.

ggplot(ler,
       aes(x=PLANTING,
           y=.data[[resp]])) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

with(ler, tapply(LER, PLANTING, t.test, mu=1))
## $early
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  X[[i]]
## t = 1.8503, df = 3, p-value = 0.1614
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 1
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.8849024 1.4348484
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
##  1.159875 
## 
## 
## $late
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  X[[i]]
## t = -0.52363, df = 3, p-value = 0.6368
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 1
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.6427853 1.2562724
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
## 0.9495289

On average LER no different from 1, surprisingly. In the early planting date it was a small increase and noise was such that we didn’t see any strong differences.

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans
t.test(ler$LER, mu=1)
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  ler$LER
## t = 0.76073, df = 7, p-value = 0.4717
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 1
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.8846674 1.2247369
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
##  1.054702

LER is not really different from 1.

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=ler)

Figure 2 - yield and LER

xlabs = c('oat' = 'Oat',
          'oat-pea' = 'Oat\nPea',
          'oat-pea-flax' = 'Oat\nPea\nFlax',
          'pea' = 'Pea')

collabs = c('oat' = 'Oat',
          'oat-pea' = 'Oat-Pea',
          'oat-pea-flax' = 'Oat-Pea-Flax',
          'pea' = 'Pea')

p1 = fig2_yield + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels=xlabs) +
  labs(tag='a)') +
  theme(legend.position='none')
p2 = fig2_biomass +
  scale_x_discrete(labels=xlabs) +
  labs(tag='b)') +
  theme(legend.position=c(0.8, 1),
        legend.key.size=unit(0.12, 'in'),
        legend.title=element_text(size=8),
        axis.ticks=element_line(color='black', linewidth=0.1), 
        axis.ticks.length=unit(0.3, units='mm'))
## Warning: A numeric `legend.position` argument in `theme()` was deprecated in ggplot2
## 3.5.0.
## ℹ Please use the `legend.position.inside` argument of `theme()` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
p3 = fig2_LER +
  labs(tag='c)') +
  scale_color_manual(values=colors,
                     labels=collabs) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(18, 17, 15, 16),
                     labels=collabs) +
  theme(legend.position='right',
        legend.key.size=unit(0.14, 'in'))
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
## Scale for shape is already present.
## Adding another scale for shape, which will replace the existing scale.
marrangeGrob(grobs=list(p1, p2, p3),
             layout_matrix=matrix(c(1,2,
                                    1,2,
                                    1,2,
                                    3,3,
                                    3,3,
                                    3,3,
                                    3,3), 
                                  nrow=7, 
                                  byrow=TRUE),
             top=NULL)
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

### Export

here(out_dir,
     'Figure 2 - Yield and LER.jpg') %>% 
  jpeg(width=5, height=4.5, units='in', res=300)

marrangeGrob(grobs=list(p1, p2, p3),
             layout_matrix=matrix(c(1,2,
                                    3,3), 
                                  nrow=2, 
                                  byrow=TRUE),
             top=NULL)
dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Stability

As differences between planting dates

keep_cols = c('REP', 'PLANTING', 'CROP', 'COMBO_YIELD_HAND')
dif = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax', select=keep_cols) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from='PLANTING',
              values_from='COMBO_YIELD_HAND')
dif$DIF = with(dif, late - early)
resp = 'DIF'
pred = 'CROP'
err = '(1|REP)'

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=dif)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

No difference in stability at this level

ggplot(dif,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=REP)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

But oat-pea difference was not different from zero.

tt = get_mean_se(DIF ~ CROP, data=dif)

tt
ttest = tapply(dif$DIF, dif$CROP, t.test) %>% 
  sapply('[[', c('p.value'))
ttest
## $oat
## [1] 0.01894229
## 
## $`oat-pea`
## [1] 0.3263896
## 
## $`oat-pea-flax`
## NULL
## 
## $pea
## [1] 0.02273904

Oat and pea yields were higher in the late planting than the early. Oat-pea was not significantly different between plantings.

Plot Fig 3 - Stability

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

grp = 'CROP'


ttest %<>% sapply(function(x) ifelse(x<0.05, '*', 'n.s.')) %>% 
  unlist(recursive=FALSE)
annotate_df = c('CROP') %>% 
  dif[, .] %>% 
  unique
annotate_df$.group = ttest[as.character(annotate_df$CROP)]

grpY = group_Y(resp, grp, data=dif)
annotate_df %<>% merge(grpY, all.x=TRUE)


pltdf = dif
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]])) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8,
               fill=colors[2]) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger,
               linewidth=0.1) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=2,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Difference in Yield [kg ha<sup>-1</sup>]<br>Planting B - Planting A',
       fill=NULL)
plt %<>% gg_formatter
  
plt

Format for export

xlabs = c('oat' = 'Oat',
          'oat-pea' = 'Oat\nPea',
          'oat-pea-flax' = 'Oat\nPea\nFlax',
          'pea' = 'Pea')
fig3 = text_sizer(plt, 8)
fig3 = fig3 + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels=xlabs)
fig3

here(out_dir,
     'Figure 3 - Stability.jpeg') %>% 
  jpeg(width=3, height=2, units='in', res=300)
fig3
dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Individual crop yields

Oat

resp = 'OAT_YIELD_HAND'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

For oats, A marginal planting date effect. Most is an interaction between mix and planting date.

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Pea

resp = 'PEA_YIELD_HAND'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

A marginal interaction between crop and planting date. Crop has a main effect, as does planting.

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)
emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Plot

pltdf = df[, c('CROP', 'PLANTING', 'OAT_YIELD_HAND', 'PEA_YIELD_HAND')] %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols=where('is.numeric'))
ggplot(pltdf,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=value,
           color=PLANTING)) +
  facet_wrap(~name) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2) +
  labs(y='Yield [kg/ha]') +
  theme_minimal()

Pea did better in the mix at the later planting date, while oats did slightly better at the earlier planting date.

Quality

Grain ratios

ratios = subset(df, CROP=='oat-pea')
ratios$RATIO = with(ratios, OAT_YIELD_HAND/PEA_YIELD_HAND)
ratios = ratios[-c(2), ]

ggplot(ratios,
       aes(x=PLANTING,
           y=RATIO)) +
  geom_point(size=2) +
  ylab("oat:pea")

mod = lmer(RATIO ~ PLANTING + (1|REP), data=ratios)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')
emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=LETTERS, reverse=TRUE)
emeans
annotate_df = emeans %>% 
  as.data.frame
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 0.5

pltdf = c('RATIO', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  ratios[, .] 
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=prettyplanting,
                 y=RATIO)) +
  # scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8,
               fill=colors[2]) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=prettyplanting,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            inherit.aes=FALSE,
            size=6,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Oat:Pea Grain Ratio [mass/mass]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt

plt = text_sizer(plt)
plt

here('Results', 'Grain Ratios Poster.jpeg') %>% 
  jpeg(height=5.5, width=6.5, units='in', res=300)
plt
dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Digestible Energy Content

resp = 'COMBO_DE'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

No interaction between crop and planting date. Crop has a main effect.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
emeans

Pea reduced energy content, unsurprisingly, as it isn’t as starchy and has a lot of fiber. Did not test for planting date effect as it wasnt’ strong enough to show up in the full test.

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 1
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]])) +#,
                 #fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Digestible Energy Content [Mcal/kg]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Digestible Energy Produced

resp = 'COMBO_DE_TOTAL'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'

# calculate total digestible energy. Mcal/ha units. 
df$COMBO_DE_TOTAL = with(df, COMBO_YIELD_HAND * COMBO_DE)
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Detectable interaction, driven by yield differences.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

No difference in energy production in early planting btw oat and oat-pea, as with yield.

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

And no consistent differences across planting dates within oat-pea, but did see planting date effects in monoculture (early lower energy produced)

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 1000
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Total Digestible Energy [Mcal/ha]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Protein Content

resp = 'COMBO_CP'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'

dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Another interaction effect.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

Oat-pea had qualitatively higher protein content.

emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING + CROP) %>% 
  pwpm
##                    early oat late oat early oat-pea late oat-pea
## early oat             [14.9]   0.9757        0.3398       0.0702
## late oat               0.615   [14.3]        0.0845       0.0152
## early oat-pea         -1.614   -2.229        [16.5]       0.9582
## late oat-pea          -2.303   -2.918        -0.689       [17.2]
## early oat-pea-flax    -1.188   -1.802         0.427        1.116
## late oat-pea-flax         NA       NA            NA           NA
## early pea            -15.795  -16.409       -14.180      -13.491
## late pea             -11.806  -12.420       -10.191       -9.502
##                    early oat-pea-flax late oat-pea-flax early pea late pea
## early oat                      0.6643                NA    <.0001   <.0001
## late oat                       0.2295                NA    <.0001   <.0001
## early oat-pea                  0.9963                NA    <.0001   <.0001
## late oat-pea                   0.7213                NA    <.0001   <.0001
## early oat-pea-flax             [16.1]                NA    <.0001   <.0001
## late oat-pea-flax                  NA            [  NA]        NA       NA
## early pea                     -14.607                NA    [30.7]   0.0008
## late pea                      -10.618                NA     3.989   [26.7]
## 
## Row and column labels: PLANTING:CROP
## Upper triangle: P values   adjust = "tukey"
## Diagonal: [Estimates] (emmean) 
## Lower triangle: Comparisons (estimate)   earlier vs. later

Crop within planting date

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Main effects

emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% pairs
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##  contrast                   estimate    SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  oat - (oat-pea)               -2.27 0.514 15.1  -4.411  0.0014
##  oat - (oat-pea-flax)         nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
##  oat - pea                    -14.11 0.514 15.1 -27.461  <.0001
##  (oat-pea) - (oat-pea-flax)   nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
##  (oat-pea) - pea              -11.84 0.514 15.1 -23.049  <.0001
##  (oat-pea-flax) - pea         nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: PLANTING 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

Planting dates within crop

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Pea produced more protein in the late planting due to higher yields despite lower protein concentration. Oat-pea showed no significant difference.

emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  pairs
## CROP = oat:
##  contrast     estimate    SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late    0.615 0.727 16.2   0.846  0.4099
## 
## CROP = oat-pea:
##  contrast     estimate    SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late   -0.689 0.727 16.2  -0.948  0.3570
## 
## CROP = oat-pea-flax:
##  contrast     estimate    SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late   nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
## 
## CROP = pea:
##  contrast     estimate    SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late    3.989 0.727 16.2   5.490  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger
prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 2
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Protein Content [g/kg]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Protein Poduced

resp = 'COMBO_CP_TOTAL'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

df[, resp] = df$COMBO_CP * df$COMBO_YIELD_HAND
ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Strong interaction between crop and planting date.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

All crops produced similar amounts of protein across planting dates, despite the very different grain ratios of the oat-pea mix.

Crop within planting date

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Main effects

emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% pwpm
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##                  oat oat-pea oat-pea-flax     pea
## oat          [36292]  0.8311           NA  0.5216
## oat-pea        -1774 [38066]           NA  0.8585
## oat-pea-flax      NA      NA      [   NA]      NA
## pea            -3383   -1609           NA [39675]
## 
## Row and column labels: CROP
## Upper triangle: P values   adjust = "tukey"
## Diagonal: [Estimates] (emmean) 
## Lower triangle: Comparisons (estimate)   earlier vs. later
emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

Planting dates within crop

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Other crops showed no planting effect on protein content.

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 1000
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Total Crude Protein [kg/ha]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Pea protein content

resp = 'PEA_CP'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'

dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Another interaction effect.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)
## Warning: Removed 8 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

Oat-pea had qualitatively higher protein content.

Crop within planting date

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Main effects

emmeans(mod, ~CROP + PLANTING) %>% pairs(p.adjust='fdr')
##  contrast                                   estimate    SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  (oat-pea early) - (oat-pea-flax early)        0.810 0.463 9.01   1.750  0.4531
##  (oat-pea early) - pea early                  -3.330 0.463 9.01  -7.198  0.0004
##  (oat-pea early) - (oat-pea late)              0.345 0.785 4.63   0.440  0.9899
##  (oat-pea early) - (oat-pea-flax late)        nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
##  (oat-pea early) - pea late                    0.659 0.785 4.63   0.840  0.9070
##  (oat-pea-flax early) - pea early             -4.139 0.463 9.01  -8.949  0.0001
##  (oat-pea-flax early) - (oat-pea late)        -0.465 0.785 4.63  -0.592  0.9704
##  (oat-pea-flax early) - (oat-pea-flax late)   nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
##  (oat-pea-flax early) - pea late              -0.150 0.785 4.63  -0.192  0.9996
##  pea early - (oat-pea late)                    3.675 0.785 4.63   4.683  0.0274
##  pea early - (oat-pea-flax late)              nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
##  pea early - pea late                          3.989 0.785 4.63   5.084  0.0196
##  (oat-pea late) - (oat-pea-flax late)         nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
##  (oat-pea late) - pea late                     0.314 0.463 9.01   0.680  0.9560
##  (oat-pea-flax late) - pea late               nonEst    NA   NA      NA      NA
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 5 estimates
emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

Planting dates within crop

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Pea protein was higher early than late, but lower in oat-pea early than pea early and higher in oat-pea late than pea late. The weather effect is straightforward to interpret.

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 2
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Protein Content [g/kg]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 8 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_summary()`).
## Removed 8 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_summary()`).
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

Oat protein content

resp = 'OAT_CP'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'

dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Marginal crop effect

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)
## Warning: Removed 8 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

Oat-pea had qualitatively higher protein content.

Main effects

emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% pwpm
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##                 oat oat-pea oat-pea-flax
## oat          [14.6]  0.7601           NA
## oat-pea      0.0964  [14.5]           NA
## oat-pea-flax     NA      NA       [  NA]
## 
## Row and column labels: CROP
## Upper triangle: P values   adjust = "tukey"
## Diagonal: [Estimates] (emmean) 
## Lower triangle: Comparisons (estimate)   earlier vs. later

Not much to see.

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 2
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Protein Content [g/kg]',

        legend.position='bottom')

## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

RFV

resp = 'COMBO_RFV'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'

dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Another interaction effect. Main effect is stronger.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

Oat-pea had qualitatively higher protein content.

Crop within planting date

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Planting dates within crop

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Main effects

emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% pwpm
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##                oat oat-pea oat-pea-flax    pea
## oat          [177]  0.1455           NA <.0001
## oat-pea        -72   [249]           NA <.0001
## oat-pea-flax    NA      NA        [ NA]     NA
## pea           -419    -347           NA  [596]
## 
## Row and column labels: CROP
## Upper triangle: P values   adjust = "tukey"
## Diagonal: [Estimates] (emmean) 
## Lower triangle: Comparisons (estimate)   earlier vs. later
emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

Higher RFV for pea than the oat-containing grain. Similar (qualitatively higher) in oat-pea than oat alone.

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 2
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Protein Content [g/kg]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

TDN

resp = 'COMBO_TDN'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

# df[, resp] = df$COMBO_CP * df$COMBO_YIELD_HAND
ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Strong interaction between crop and planting date.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

All crops produced similar amounts of protein across planting dates, despite the very different grain ratios of the oat-pea mix.

Main effects

emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% pwpm
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##                 oat oat-pea oat-pea-flax    pea
## oat          [76.3]  0.4849           NA <.0001
## oat-pea      -0.942  [77.2]           NA <.0001
## oat-pea-flax     NA      NA       [  NA]     NA
## pea          -6.134  -5.192           NA [82.4]
## 
## Row and column labels: CROP
## Upper triangle: P values   adjust = "tukey"
## Diagonal: [Estimates] (emmean) 
## Lower triangle: Comparisons (estimate)   earlier vs. later
emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

Higher TDN in pea than oat-contining.

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 10
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Total Digestible Nutrients [% mass]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

TDN Per Area

resp = 'COMBO_TDN_AREA'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

df[, resp] = df$COMBO_TDN * df$COMBO_YIELD_HAND/100
ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Strong interaction between crop and planting date.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

All crops produced similar amounts of protein across planting dates, despite the very different grain ratios of the oat-pea mix.

Crop within planting date

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP | PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Planting dates within crop

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Main effects

emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% pwpm
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##                 oat oat-pea oat-pea-flax    pea
## oat          [1902]  0.3437           NA <.0001
## oat-pea         168  [1734]           NA 0.0007
## oat-pea-flax     NA      NA       [  NA]     NA
## pea             724     556           NA [1178]
## 
## Row and column labels: CROP
## Upper triangle: P values   adjust = "tukey"
## Diagonal: [Estimates] (emmean) 
## Lower triangle: Comparisons (estimate)   earlier vs. later
emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

But higher total nutrients in oat and oat-pea

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 100
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Total Digestible Nutrients [kg/ha]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

ADF

resp = 'COMBO_ADF'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

# df[, resp] = df$COMBO_CP * df$COMBO_YIELD_HAND
ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Strong interaction between crop and planting date.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

All crops produced similar amounts of protein across planting dates, despite the very different grain ratios of the oat-pea mix.

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
emeans

Other crops showed no planting effect on protein content.

emmeans(mod, ~ PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  pairs
## CROP = oat:
##  contrast     estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late   -2.438 1.43 16.3  -1.701  0.1081
## 
## CROP = oat-pea:
##  contrast     estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late    0.817 1.43 16.3   0.570  0.5764
## 
## CROP = oat-pea-flax:
##  contrast     estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late   nonEst   NA   NA      NA      NA
## 
## CROP = pea:
##  contrast     estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late    0.550 1.43 16.3   0.384  0.7061
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger
prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 3
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Acid Detergent Fiber [% mass]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

NDF

resp = 'COMBO_NDF'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

# df[, resp] = df$COMBO_CP * df$COMBO_YIELD_HAND
ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Strong interaction between crop and planting date.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

All crops produced similar amounts of protein across planting dates, despite the very different grain ratios of the oat-pea mix.

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
emeans

Other crops showed no planting effect on protein content.

emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  pairs
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##  contrast                   estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  oat - (oat-pea)                6.14 2.21 15.1   2.780  0.0353
##  oat - (oat-pea-flax)         nonEst   NA   NA      NA      NA
##  oat - pea                     27.05 2.21 15.1  12.244  <.0001
##  (oat-pea) - (oat-pea-flax)   nonEst   NA   NA      NA      NA
##  (oat-pea) - pea               20.91 2.21 15.1   9.464  <.0001
##  (oat-pea-flax) - pea         nonEst   NA   NA      NA      NA
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: PLANTING 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING | CROP) %>% 
  pairs
## CROP = oat:
##  contrast     estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late    -5.51 3.12 16.2  -1.763  0.0968
## 
## CROP = oat-pea:
##  contrast     estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late     0.78 3.12 16.2   0.250  0.8060
## 
## CROP = oat-pea-flax:
##  contrast     estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late   nonEst   NA   NA      NA      NA
## 
## CROP = pea:
##  contrast     estimate   SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  early - late     3.42 3.12 16.2   1.094  0.2900
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger
prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 10
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Neutral Detergent Fiber [% mass]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

Exact means and standard errors are:

get_mean_se(paste(resp, pred, sep='~') %>% formula,
            data=df)

      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')

emeans
emmeans(mod, ~CROP) %>% 
  pairs
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##  contrast                   estimate     SE   df t.ratio p.value
##  oat - (oat-pea)              0.0683 0.0406 15.1   1.680  0.2447
##  oat - (oat-pea-flax)         nonEst     NA   NA      NA      NA
##  oat - pea                    0.3912 0.0406 15.1   9.633  <.0001
##  (oat-pea) - (oat-pea-flax)   nonEst     NA   NA      NA      NA
##  (oat-pea) - pea              0.3230 0.0406 15.1   7.952  <.0001
##  (oat-pea-flax) - pea         nonEst     NA   NA      NA      NA
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: PLANTING 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 0.5
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Metabolizable Energy [kcal/kg?]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
  
plt

annotate_df$Y = .1
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>%

  as.character %>%

  as.character %>%

dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,

  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Net Energy for Lactation [kcal/kg]',
       fill=NULL) +

        legend.position='bottom')

NEl_Area

resp = 'COMBO_NEl_AREA'
pred = 'CROP*PLANTING'
err = '(1|REP/PLANTING)'
dd = subset(df, CROP != 'oat-pea-flax')

df[, resp] = df$COMBO_NEl * df$COMBO_YIELD_HAND
ff = paste(resp, '~', pred, '+', err) %>% 
  formula

mod = lmer(ff, data=df)
Anova(mod, test.statistic='F')

Strong interaction between crop and planting date.

ggplot(df,
       aes(x=CROP,
           y=.data[[resp]],
           color=PLANTING)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, 
             size=2)

All crops produced similar amounts of protein across planting dates, despite the very different grain ratios of the oat-pea mix.

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~CROP|PLANTING) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

Oat-pea no different from oat alone both times.

emeans = emmeans(mod, ~PLANTING|CROP) %>% 
  cld(Letters=letters,
      reverse=TRUE,
      p.adjust='fdr')
emeans

oat-pea stable across weather.

prettyplanting = c(early = 'Planting A',
                   late = 'Planting B')

annotate_df = c('CROP', 'PLANTING') %>% 
  df[, .] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  merge(emeans)
annotate_df$.group %<>% trimws()
annotate_df$Y = 100
annotate_df[annotate_df$CROP=='oat-pea-flax', '.group'] = NA

pltdf = c('CROP', 'PLANTING', resp) %>% 
  df[, .]
late_flax = data.frame(CROP='oat-pea-flax' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$CROP)),
               PLANTING='late' %>% 
                 factor(levels=levels(df$PLANTING)),
               x=0) %>% 
  set_names(names(pltdf))
pltdf %<>% rbind(late_flax)
               
pltdf$prettyplanting = pltdf$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
annotate_df$prettyplanting = annotate_df$PLANTING %>% 
  as.character %>% 
  prettyplanting[.]
dodger = position_dodge(width=0.8)

plt = ggplot(pltdf, 
             aes(x=CROP,
                 y=.data[[resp]],
                 fill=prettyplanting)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors[3:4]) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean,
               geom='bar',
               position=dodger,
               width=0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se,
               geom='errorbar',
               width=0,
               position=dodger) +
  geom_text(data=annotate_df,
            aes(x=CROP,
                y=Y,
                label=.group),
            size=8,
            position=dodger) +
  labs(x=NULL,
       y='Net Energy for Lactation [kcal/ha]',
       fill=NULL) +
  theme(panel.background=element_blank(),
        legend.position='bottom')
